title: [Bug]: `case when` with subquery may produce unexpected result
description: `case when` with subquery may produce unexpected result
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t_test_case (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, value DOUBLE, description VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO t_test_case (id, value, description) VALUES (1, 123.45, 'Test case 1'), (2, NULL, 'Test case 2'), (3, 678.90, 'Test case 3');
SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM t_test_case WHERE value IS NOT NULL) > 1 THEN 'Multiple values' ELSE 'Single value' END AS result;
DELETE FROM t_test_case WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM t_test_case WHERE value IS NULL);
CREATE TABLE t_subquery_test AS SELECT id, value FROM t_test_case WHERE value > (SELECT AVG(value) FROM t_test_case);
key_bug_features: `case when` with subquery produces unexpected results
